title: Rondeau. - It Might Have Been.
content:
It might have been so different a year
    To what has been; the summer's guileless play
    Not all a jest, comes back to me to-day
    In add

title: Misunderstanding.
content:
Spring's face is wreathed in smiles. She had been driven
        Hither and thither at the surly will
        Of treacherous winds till her swee

title: Barter
content:
There is a long thin line of fading gold
        In the far West, and the transfigured leaves
        On some slight, topmost bough that sways a

title: An Evening In October
content:
Evening has thrown her hushing garment round
    This little world; no harsh or jarring sound
    Disturbs my reverie. The room is dark,
    An

title: Doubt.
content:
I do not know if all the fault be mine,
        Or why I may not think of thee and be
        At peace with mine own heart. Unceasingly
    Gri

title: Parted.
content:
My spirit holds you, Dear,
    Though worlds away," - 
    This to their absent ones
    Many can say.

    "Thoughts, fancies, hopes, desires

title: Tout Pour L'Amour.
There Is No God.
content:
There is no God? If one should stand at noon
    Where the glow rests, and the warm sunlight plays,
    Where earth is gladdened by the cordial

title: Disappointment.
content:
The light has left the hill-side. Yesterday
    These skies shewed blue against the dusky trees,
    The leaves' soft murmur in the evening bree

title: Rondeau. - When Summer Comes.
content:
When summer comes, and when o'er hill and lea
    The sun's strong wooing glow hath patiently
    Shed o'er the earth long days his golden dower

title: Dream-Song.
content:
Cam'st thou not nigh to me
    In that one glimpse of thee
    When thy lips, tremblingly,
            Said: "My Beloved."
    'Twas but a mom

title: Shadow Song.
content:
The night is long
        And there are no stars, - 
            Let me but dream
            That the long fields gleam
    With sunlight and

title: Soothing.
content:
I aimless wandered thro' the woods, and flung
    My idle limbs upon a soft brown bank,
    Where, thickly strewn, the worn-out russet leaves

title: Revulsion.
content:
I see the starting buds, I catch the gleam
        In the near distance of a sun-kissed pool,
        The blessed April air blows soft and cool,

title: Echoes.
content:
A breath                         A breath
       And a sigh, -                     And a sigh, - 
        How we fly                       How w

title: To-Morrow.
content:
But one short night between my Love and me!
        I watch the soft-shod dusk creep wistfully
        Through the slow-moving curtains, pausing

title: Pictures.
content:
The full-orbed Paschal moon; dark shadows flung
    On the brown Lenten earth; tall spectral trees
    Stand in their huge and naked strength er

title: Slack Tide.
content:
My boat is still in the reedy cove
    Where the rushes hinder its onward course,
    For I care not now if we rest or move
    O'er the slumbe

title: Crows.
content:
They stream across the fading western sky
    A sable cloud, far o'er the lonely leas;
    Now parting into scattered companies,
    Now closin

title: Eurydice.
content:
Oh come, Eurydice!
    The Stygian deeps are past
    Well-nigh; the light dawns fast.
    Oh come, Eurydice!

    The gods have heard my song

title: Rondeau. - I Will Forget.
content:
I will forget those days of mingled bliss
    And dear delicious pain, - will cast from me
    All dreams of what I know can never be,
    Even

title: Triumph.
content:
The sky, grown dull through many waiting days,
    Flashed into crimson with the sunrise charm,
    So all my love, aroused to vague alarm,

title: Death.
content:
If days should pass without a written word
        To tell me of thy welfare, and if days
        Should lengthen out to weeks, until the maze

title: Rondeau. - For Our Love's Sake.
content:
For our Love's sake I bid thee stay,
    Sweet, ere the hours flee away,
    Beneath the old acacia tree
    That waves its blossoms quiveringl

title: Battle Song.
content:
Clear sounds the call on high:
    "To arms and victory!"
    Brave hearts that win or die,
            Dying, may win;
    Proudly the banner

title: Noon.
content:
No ripple stirs the water,
    No song-bird wakes the grove,
    Calm noon-tide sways his sceptre,
    And hushes even love.

    On earth the

title: Incompleteness.
content:
Since first I met thee, Dear, and long before
        I knew myself beloved, save by the sense
        All women have, a shadowy confidence

title: Song.
content:
If I had known
    That when the morrow dawned the roses would be dead
    I would have filled my hands with blossoms white and red.

title: Gratitude.
content:
There are some things, dear Friend, are easier far
        To say in written words than when we sit
        Eye answering eye, or hand to hand c

title: Life's Joys.
content:
I have been pondering what our teachers call
        The mystery of Pain; and lo! my thought
        After it's half-blind reaching out has caug

title: Anticipation.
content:
Let us peer forward through the dusk of years
        And force the silent future to reveal
        Her store of garnered joys; we may not kneel

title: Weariness.
content:
This April sun has wakened into cheer
        The wintry paths of thought, and tinged with gold
        These threadbare leaves of fancy brown a

title: Sea-Song.
content:
A dash of spray,
    A weed-browned way, - 
    My ship's in the bay,
    In the glad blue bay, - 
    The wind's from the west
    And the w

title: Content.
content:
I have been wandering where the daisies grow,
        Great fields of tall, white daisies, and I saw
        Them bend reluctantly, and seem to

title: Futurity.
content:
What of our life when this frail flesh lies low
    A withered clod, and the free soul has burst
    Through the world-fetters? Not of souls acc

title: A Song of Rest.
A Dream.
content:
I stood far off above the haunts of men
        Somewhere, I know not, when the sky was dim
        From some worn glory, and the morning hymn

title: Rondeau. - Brother And Friend.
content:
Brother and friend I found thee in the hour
    Of need and day of trouble, strong and true. - 
    In June's fair mirth, and when the sunrise h

title: Rondeau. - Pourquoi?
content:
"Pourquoi," she breathed, then drooped her head,
    (Pure snow-drifts to the sunset wed)
    As all my weakness I confessed.
    I shewed how

title: A Song of Dawn.
Prayer.
content:
I stood upon a hill, and watched the death
        Of the day's turmoil. Still the glory spread
        Cloud-top to cloud-top, and each rearing

title: Loneliness.
content:
Dear, I am lonely, for the bay is still
        As any hill-girt lake; the long brown beach
        Lies bare and wet. As far as eye can reach

title: A Shadow.
content:
The world to-day is radiant, as I ne'er
    Could picture it in wildest dreaming, when
    For long, long hours I lay in flowery glen
    Or wo
